title: eremophila denticulata
intro: Eremophila denticulata, also known as the 'Gum Flower', is a flowering plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its bright yellow, orange, or red flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, Eremophila denticulata has been used in indigenous Australian medicine, its leaves and stems containing iridoid glycosides with demonstrated antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and may also have antioxidant and anti-diabetic effects. Eremophila denticulata can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion to harness its medicinal properties.
